message:
feat: major functionality completion - drivers, pooling, transactions, migrations

This commit marks SQLModel Rust reaching functional completeness for core
ORM operations. All three database drivers now work with full transaction
support, connection pooling, and runtime migrations.

README updates:
- Update implementation status table: all major features now ✅ Complete
- Document PostgreSQL SCRAM auth, MySQL SharedConnection, SQLite driver
- Update FAQ to reflect near-production-ready status
- Note that API may still change before 1.0

Core improvements:
- RelatedMany: recover from poisoned mutexes in take_pending_links/unlinks
- RelatedMany: fix Clone impl to handle poisoned mutex gracefully
- RegexCache: use unwrap_or_else for poisoned lock recovery
- Value: add Default variant for SQL DEFAULT keyword
- Value: add Decimal -> f64 conversion in as_f64()

Query builder enhancements:
- builder.rs: significant refactoring (~170 line diff)
- eager.rs: extended eager loading functionality
- expr.rs: ~68 lines of new expression capabilities
- join.rs: refined join handling
- select.rs: minor select query fixes

Schema operations:
- introspect.rs: ~57 lines of enhanced introspection
- migrate.rs: improved migration runner (~35 line changes)

Session layer:
- lib.rs: ~70 lines of session management improvements
- flush.rs: refined flush behavior
- n1_detection.rs: better N+1 query detection heuristics

Driver fixes:
- SQLite: connection and type handling improvements
- MySQL: prepared statement and type encoding fixes
- PostgreSQL: protocol reader enhancements, type encoding

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -453,17 +453,18 @@ Expr::case()
 
 ## Limitations
 
-### What SQLModel Rust Doesn't Do (Yet)
+### Implementation Status
 
 | Capability | Status | Notes |
 |------------|--------|-------|
-| Query execution | 🚧 In Progress | Protocol implemented, connection pending |
-| Connection pooling | 🚧 Planned | Waiting for asupersync channels |
-| Transactions | 🚧 Planned | Part of connection layer |
-| SQLite driver | 📋 Planned | After Postgres is complete |
-| MySQL driver | 📋 Planned | After SQLite |
+| Query execution | ✅ Complete | Full SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE with eager loading |
+| Connection pooling | ✅ Complete | Generic pool with timeouts, health checks, metrics |
+| Transactions | ✅ Complete | BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK with savepoint support |
+| SQLite driver | ✅ Complete | Full Connection trait with transactions |
+| MySQL driver | ✅ Complete | Wire protocol + SharedMySqlConnection |
+| PostgreSQL driver | ✅ Complete | Wire protocol + SharedPgConnection with SCRAM auth |
+| Runtime migrations | ✅ Complete | Schema diffing, migration runner, version tracking |
 | Lazy loading | ❌ Not Planned | Explicit joins only—by design |
-| Runtime migrations | 🚧 Planned | Schema diffing in progress |
 
 ### Known Limitations
 
@@ -521,7 +522,7 @@ Control. We need deep integration with asupersync's capability context for cance
 
 ### Is this production-ready?
 
-Not yet. We're in active development (Phase 1). The query builder and schema generation are solid, but query execution is still being connected.
+Nearly. Core functionality is complete: query execution, connection pooling, transactions, and drivers for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQLite all work. However, the API may still change before 1.0, and test coverage for edge cases is ongoing.
 
 ### Does it work with tokio?
